initial_draft:
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of X. My production cost was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell for more than 40 ZUP. Player BLUE offered to buy with ZUP. I will propose an initial high price to test their willingness to pay. A price of 60 ZUP is a reasonable starting point, giving a 20 ZUP profit margin while leaving room for negotiation. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I have 1 unit of X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 60 ZUP. Let me know if this is acceptable or if you would like to negotiate a different price. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All required tags (`<proposal count>`, `<my resources>`, `<my goals>`, `<reason>`, `<player answer>`, `<newly proposed trade>`, `<message>`) are present and in the correct order. The trade syntax is valid.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Selling for 60 ZUP against a 40 ZUP cost yields a 20 ZUP profit, which satisfies the goal of selling for a profit.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. Since the user (Player BLUE) has not made a specific counter-offer yet, setting an initial anchor price of 60 ZUP is a standard negotiation tactic to see their reaction. It is plausible.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason explicitly states the intent to propose a high price, which matches the PROPOSAL action and the specific numbers in the trade. The message also aligns with the proposal.
5. Rule compliance: OK. This is the first proposal (count 1), well within the limit of 3 proposals.
</critique>

2. Payoff alignment: OK. The proposed trade yields 20 ZUP profit (60 - 40), which maximizes utility given the current negotiation stance.

5. Rule compliance: OK. This is the first proposal, well within the 3-proposal limit.
</critique>
